perturbation.original_text: Party B may use the Program Content licensed by Party A only in Party B Business. Without Party A's consent in writing, Party B may not in any way use the Program Content provided by Party A for any purposes other than in connection with Party B Business, nor may Party B permit any third party to use in any way the Program Content licensed by Party A to Party B prior to the publishing of the Program Content on the Phoenix Satellite TV Websites or Other Websites.
perturbation.changed_text: Party B may use the Program Content licensed by Party A only in Party B Business. Without Party A's consent in writing, Party B may not in any way use the Licensed Material provided by Party A for any purposes other than in connection with Party B Business, nor may Party B permit any third party to use in any way the Program Content licensed by Party A to Party B prior to the publishing of the Program Content on the Phoenix Satellite TV Websites or Other Websites.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Program Content' is now being used interchangeably with 'Licensed Material' in Article 2.1. This is creating an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether all 'Program Content' is considered 'Licensed Material' or if they are different entities within the agreement. The text refers that only the Program Content licensed by Party A can be called Licensed Material. This introduces uncertainty as it suggests there might be Program Content not licensed, or that its usage rights differ. Also, Party B Business and Phoenix Satellite TV Websites or Other Websites are not being taken in consideration when referring to Licensed Material
perturbation.contradicted_text: Party B may use the Program Content licensed by Party A only in Party B Business. Without Party A's consent in writing, Party B may not in any way use the Licensed Material provided by Party A for any purposes other than in connection with Party B Business, nor may Party B permit any third party to use in any way the Program Content licensed by Party A to Party B prior to the publishing of the Program Content on the Phoenix Satellite TV Websites or Other Websites.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Licensed Material' in place of 'Program Content' in a section that also uses 'Program Content,' leading to confusion. The explanation points out that the interchangeable use of 'Program Content' and 'Licensed Material' creates ambiguity, particularly regarding the scope of 'Licensed Material' and its relation to Party B Business, Phoenix Satellite TV Websites, and other Websites, which contradicts the original intent and creates a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Program Content during its use of the same, Party B shall notify Party A and, upon its request in writing, sign all documents and take all actions required to assign such Intellectual Property Right to Party A, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained by Party A is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.changed_text: Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Licensed Material during its use of the same, Party B shall notify Party A and, upon its request in writing, sign all documents and take all actions required to assign such Intellectual Property Right to Party A, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained by Party A is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation replaces 'Program Content' with 'Licensed Material' in the second sentence of Article 5.1. This introduces uncertainty because it's unclear if the obligation to notify and assign Intellectual Property Rights only applies to 'Licensed Material' or to all 'Program Content,' potentially creating ambiguity if Party B develops something based on Program Content not explicitly considered licensed material. Thus, if Party B creates any derivative work from licensed material, Party A obtains all the rights. If it does from not-licensed-material-program-content, it obtains rights from Program content, but is ambiguous if there will be a share in the newly produced IP.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both Parties acknowledge and agree that with respect to Program Content licensed to Party B hereunder, Party B shall not have any copyright or any other Intellectual Property Right. If Party B obtains any Intellectual Property Right in respect of the Licensed Material during its use of the same, Party B shall notify Party A and, upon its request in writing, sign all documents and take all actions required to assign such Intellectual Property Right to Party A, and ensure the Intellectual Property Right so obtained by Party A is legitimate, complete, and free from any encumbrance.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Licensed Material' where 'Program Content' was originally used. The original text made it clear that any IP obtained in respect to the Program Content during Party B's use must be assigned to Party A. However, with the change, only IP obtained from 'Licensed Material' triggers this obligation. This creates a contradiction because it's now unclear what happens to IP derived from Program Content that isn't explicitly considered 'Licensed Material', leading to uncertainty and potential conflict. The changed text introduces ambiguity and contradicts the implicit assumption in the original text that all IP related to Program Content falls under Party A's ownership.

perturbation.original_text: When providing the Program Content to Party B, Party A may specify the special purpose for which such Program Content shall be used, the extent to which such Program Content shall be transmitted, the time or times at which such Program Content shall be transmitted (including the time at which such Program Content is published on the Phoenix Satellite TV Website or Other
perturbation.changed_text: When providing the Program Content to Party B, Party A may specify the special purpose for which such Program Content shall be used, the extent to which such Program Content shall be transmitted, the time or times at which such Licensed Material shall be transmitted (including the time at which such Program Content is published on the Phoenix Satellite TV Website or Other
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies Article 6.1 by substituting 'Program Content' with 'Licensed Material' concerning the transmission times. It creates ambiguity because it implies only the transmission time of Licensed Material needs to be specified, while the original sentence refers to Program Content as well, thus suggesting the usage and extent of Program Content are still being specified by Party A. In-text contradiction arises by not explicitly clarifying that Party B, has to fulfill Party A requirements on when the Program Content is published on the Phoenix Satellite TV Website or Other Websites; creating uncertainty on the transmission times in cases of doubt of the definition
perturbation.contradicted_text: When providing the Program Content to Party B, Party A may specify the special purpose for which such Program Content shall be used, the extent to which such Program Content shall be transmitted, the time or times at which such Licensed Material shall be transmitted (including the time at which such Program Content is published on the Phoenix Satellite TV Website or Other
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Program Content' with 'Licensed Material' when referring to transmission times. The explanation states this creates ambiguity because it implies only the transmission time of Licensed Material needs to be specified, while the original sentence refers to Program Content as well. It creates uncertainty on the transmission times in cases of doubt of the definition. Therefore it contradicts with the Contradicted Text as it breaks compatibility.
